architecture.summary: IPR is a training framework for LLM agents that uses Monte Carlo step-level reward estimation to provide process supervision. During each iteration, the agent explores along expert trajectories and generates new actions. These actions are evaluated against expert trajectory steps using step-level rewards, producing contrastive action pairs (agent vs. expert actions at each step) that serve as training data for the agent. The system does not modify the agent's architecture but rather provides iterative refinement of its behavior through step-wise contrastive learning.
trained_components: agent policy (via contrastive step-level training data)

biblio.title: Watch Every Step! LLM Agent Learning via Iterative Step-level Process Refinement

claims.statement: IPR framework provides detailed step-by-step guidance to enhance agent training
IPR adopts the Monte Carlo method to estimate step-level rewards
During each iteration, the agent explores along the expert trajectory and generates new actions
Actions are evaluated against the corresponding step of expert trajectory using step-level rewards, yielding contrastive action pairs that serve as training data
IPR framework outperforms a variety of strong baselines on three complex agent tasks
IPR is effective in augmenting action efficiency and is applicable to diverse models
